issues: Authorization logic uses && (AND) instead of || (OR) when checking isTeamAdmin and isTeamOwner, meaning a user must be both admin and owner simultaneously. Team admins who aren't owners are incorrectly blocked from the feature.

When booking.eventType?.teamId is null/undefined, a fallback value of 0 is passed to isTeamAdmin and isTeamOwner. A teamId of 0 almost certainly doesn't exist and needs a proper null check and early skip.
No booking status check exists in the addGuests handler, allowing guests to be added to cancelled or rejected bookings.
No past booking guard exists, allowing guests to be added to bookings that ended months ago with no startTime > now() check.
The handler ignores the eventType.guestsEnabled feature flag, bypassing the organizer's explicit setting controlling whether guests are allowed.

Client-side and server-side validation schemas are defined separately with different rules. The client checks uniqueness but the server doesn't, and the client-side schema is recreated on every render inside the component body.
Email comparison does not normalize case. Emails are case-insensitive per RFC 5321, so 'John@Example.com' and 'john@example.com' pass the duplicate check, creating duplicate attendees.
The blacklist lowercases its entries but incoming guest emails are never lowercased, so uppercase variants like 'BLOCKED@EXAMPLE.COM' bypass the blacklist.

sendAddGuestsEmails does not check organization email settings, eventTypeDisableHostEmail, or shouldSkipAttendeeEmailWithSettings, unconditionally sending emails to everyone and breaking customer configurations.
The catch block around sendAddGuestsEmails uses console.log instead of console.error and completely ignores the captured err object, providing zero diagnostic information in logs.
The 40+ line CalendarEvent construction block is nearly identical to editLocation.handler.ts and should be extracted into a shared utility like buildCalendarEventFromBooking().
The MultiEmail component reassigns a destructured prop with 'value = value || []', mutating a parameter.
MultiEmail uses key={index} for list items that can be added/removed, causing inputs to shift incorrectly when deleting items.
The tooltip content 'Remove email' is hardcoded in English in a fully-localized codebase.
IDs and test IDs use 'add_members' instead of 'add_guests', which are different concepts in cal.com and will mislead anyone searching the codebase.
Ternaries on startTime and endTime are dead code since these fields are non-nullable on Booking.
'Nameless' is a magic string that should be a translation key or constant.
